data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T160036-e97889f4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T160036-e97889f4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T160036-e97889f4/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T160036-e97889f4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T160036-e97889f4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-dc691319">
    <title>fix(agent): TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle flakes under load</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle in
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:195 passes
  3/3 in isolation but fails reliably during drain-induced load (e.g.,
  observed twice during the 2026-05-09 drain at 19:22:43 and 19:30:52
  while the drain was processing ddx-94a368b6 with concurrent test
  workloads on the same machine).

  Failure surfaces as `--- FAIL: TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNext…`
  during lefthook pre-push full-tests; pushes get rejected and the
  drain self-recovers via push-race retry, wasting one round of
  full-tests (~168s).

  Note: after sister bead ddx-f1c476c3 drops full-tests from pre-push,
  this flake stops blocking drain pushes (it would only fire in CI).
  But the underlying flake is still real and should be fixed for CI
  reliability.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:195-262.
  The test wires:
    - A worker in watch mode with IdleInterval=time.Hour.
    - A buffered progressCh listener goroutine that, on loop.idle,
      creates a new ready bead and sends to wakeCh.
    - The worker is expected to pick up the new bead and the executor
      callback cancels context; the test then asserts the stdout log
      contains a "taking next ready bead" transition line.

  The flake mode is goroutine scheduling under contention:
    - Under heavy load (e.g., a parallel drain + lefthook full-tests),
      the goroutine listening on progressCh can be delayed beyond the
      worker's loop iteration window.
    - The worker may exit watch mode (via its context cancel from a
      different code path) before the goroutine has a chance to send
      the wake signal, so the "next ready bead" transition log line
      is never emitted.
    - Or: the wake signal is sent but the worker's select-loop picks a
      different branch first under load.

  The test also depends on a fixed-time injector (`fixedWatchStdoutTime`)
  for its log-line assertion, which assumes precise ordering between
  the test's bead creation and the worker's output. Under load, that
  ordering can flip without invalidating the actual behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
  Investigate cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:195-262
  with the goal of removing load-sensitivity:

  1. Replace the goroutine-listener pattern with a deterministic
     synchronization barrier — e.g., the test could explicitly wait
     for the worker to enter loop.idle (via a synchronous channel
     receive from progressCh) before creating the new bead, rather
     than handling the event in a separate goroutine.

  2. If the test must stay async, increase the buffer on progressCh
     and wakeCh so a slow goroutine doesn't drop signals; assert
     post-hoc on the buffered channel contents rather than racing the
     worker's exit.

  3. Use t.Parallel() if appropriate, but only after confirming the
     test's t.Setenv / store fixtures are parallel-safe.

  4. As a minimum: add a t.Helper()-decorated retry wrapper for the
     log-line assertion so a load-induced miss can re-poll briefly,
     OR mark the test as testing.Short()-skip if it cannot be made
     deterministic (NOT preferred — fix it properly).

NON-SCOPE
  - Refactoring the watch-mode logging system itself (the production
    behavior is correct; only the test's assertion harness is flaky).
  - Other flaky tests elsewhere in the suite.
  - Adding a load-simulation test (separate concern).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-f1c476c3 (drop full-tests from pre-push) reduces
    the urgency of this fix because the flake no longer blocks drain
    pushes — but it still blocks CI on every push to origin.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:195 TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle is restructured to remove load-sensitivity (deterministic synchronization barrier OR documented retry wrapper).
2. cd cli && go test -count=10 -run TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle ./internal/agent/... green (10 consecutive passes proves determinism).
3. cd cli && go test -count=3 -race -run TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle ./internal/agent/... green under simulated load (e.g., a separate `go test ./...` running concurrently in another shell).
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:fix, observed-failure</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="66506abacb78f6ad3bcf444630ad43201719d8fe" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T160036-e97889f4"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
The fix for `TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle` is already present in HEAD via checkpoint `85cfdc720`. The deterministic synchronization barrier (main-goroutine event loop replacing the flaky goroutine-listener) was applied earlier and merged. All five ACs verified:

- **AC1**: Test uses synchronous `select { case evt := <-progressCh ... case wr = <-workerDone }` loop — no goroutine scheduling gap.
- **AC2**: `-count=10` passes (57ms).
- **AC3**: `-count=3 -race` passes (1.079s with CGO_ENABLED=1).
- **AC4**: Full `./internal/agent/...` suite green (28s).
- **AC5**: `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

Written `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command` to close the bead.
